textPlain: Disturbing that they would be proud enough of spying on their users to post this. Threat intelligence is nearly as bad as the threats themselves. From crowdstrike destroying computer systems to this type of spying on their own users, who wants to trust these people? What happened to holding microsoft accountable for the security  of their products?

I don't work in cybersecurity and, after looking at the site's homepage, couldn't exactly figure out from all the buzzwords what exactly is this product. The most concerning takeaway from this article for me is that the maintainers of Huntress (whatever it is) can keep a log of, as well as personally access, the users' browser history, history of launched executables, device's hostname, and presumably a lot of other information. How is this product not a total security nightmare?

It looks like Huntress is a "install this on your computer and we'll watch over your systems and keep you safe, for sure."I also find it kind of funny that the "blunder" mentioned in the title, according to the article is ... installing Huntress's agent. Do they look at every customer's google searches to see if they're suspicious too?
